=== Metadata ===
Effect version: 3.19.19

/.src/globalConsole.ts(5,1): warning TS377064: This code uses `console.log`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.log or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)
/.src/globalConsole.ts(9,3): warning TS377064: This code uses `console.warn`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.logWarning or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)
/.src/globalConsole.ts(14,1): warning TS377064: This code uses `console.info`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.logInfo or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)
/.src/globalConsole.ts(28,20): warning TS377064: This code uses `console.error`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.logError or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)


==== /.src/globalConsole.ts (4 errors) ====
    // @effect-diagnostics globalConsole:warning
    import { Effect } from "effect"
    
    // Should trigger - console.log at module level
    console.log("module level")
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377064: This code uses `console.log`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.log or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)
    
    // Should trigger - console.warn in regular function
    const _regularFn = () => {
      console.warn("warning")
      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377064: This code uses `console.warn`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.logWarning or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)
    }
    
    // Should trigger - aliased console at module level
    const myConsole = console
    myConsole.info("info")
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377064: This code uses `console.info`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.logInfo or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)
    
    // Should NOT trigger - console.log inside Effect.gen
    export const consoleLogInGen = Effect.gen(function*() {
      console.log("hello")
    })
    
    // Should NOT trigger - console.warn inside Effect.fn
    export const consoleWarnInFn = Effect.fn("consoleWarnInFn")(function*() {
      console.warn("warning")
    })
    
    // Should trigger - console.error inside nested arrow in generator
    export const nestedArrowInGen = Effect.gen(function*() {
      const fn = () => console.error("nested")
                       ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377064: This code uses `console.error`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.logError or Logger`. effect(globalConsole)
      return fn
    })
    
    // Should NOT trigger - shadowed console
    const _shadowedConsole = () => {
      const console = { log: (..._args: Array<unknown>) => undefined }
      console.log("hello")
    }
